Class HiddenChainAttackBehaviorParameterTest

java.lang.Object
dom.institution.lab.cns.bitcoin.node.HiddenChainAttackBehaviorParameterTest
public class HiddenChainAttackBehaviorParameterTest extends Object
JUnit 5 test class for HiddenChainAttackBehavior.

Tests contract violations and precondition enforcement for the hidden chain attack strategy. Focuses on:

  • Constructor preconditions (node cannot be null)
  • Parameter configuration and validation
  • State transition contract violations
  • Attack condition preconditions

See Also:
  • HiddenChainAttackBehavior
  • HonestNodeBehavior

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    setUp()
     
    void
    testConstructor_InitializesToIdleState()
    Tests that constructor initializes to IDLE state.
    void
    testConstructor_NullNode_ThrowsNullPointerException()
    Tests that constructor throws NullPointerException when node is null.
    void
    testGoToIdleState_AlreadyIdle_ThrowsIllegalStateException()
    Tests that goToIdleState() throws when already in IDLE state.
    void
    testGoToIdleState_GuardsAgainstInvalidTransitions()
    Tests that goToIdleState() throws when called inappropriately.
    void
    testGoToMonitoringState_AlreadyMonitoring_ThrowsIllegalStateException()
    Tests that goToMonitoringState() throws when already in MONITORING state.
    void
    testGoToMonitoringState_GuardsAgainstInvalidTransitions()
    Tests that goToMonitoringState() throws when in ATTACKING state.
    void
    testInHiddenChain_AfterCancelAttack_ReturnsFalse()
    After the attack is cancelled the hidden chain is cleared, so inHiddenChain() must return false for any transaction.
    void
    testInHiddenChain_MonitoringState_ReturnsFalse()
    When in MONITORING state (attack not yet started, hidden chain empty), inHiddenChain() must return false.
    void
    testInHiddenChain_NoAttackInProgress_ReturnsFalse()
    When no attack is in progress (hiddenChainTip is null), inHiddenChain() must return false for any transaction regardless of its ID.
    void
    testInHiddenChain_TransactionInTipBlock_ReturnsTrue()
    A transaction contained in the single hidden block at the tip must be found.
    void
    testInHiddenChain_TransactionNotInAnyBlock_ReturnsFalse()
    A transaction that has never been seen by the node must not be found in the hidden chain, even during an active attack.
    void
    testSetAttackPower_NonPositive_ThrowsIllegalArgumentException()
    Tests that setAttackPower() throws when power is invalid input: '<'= 0.
    void
    testSetAttackPower_Positive_Succeeds()
    Tests that setAttackPower() accepts positive values.
    void
    testSetReleaseAdvantage_AcceptsAnyValue()
    Tests that setReleaseAdvantage() accepts any value.
    void
    testStateTransition_IdleToMonitoring_Succeeds()
    Tests that valid state transition IDLE → MONITORING succeeds.
    void
    testStateTransition_MonitoringToIdle_Succeeds()
    Tests that valid state transition MONITORING → IDLE succeeds.

  • Constructor Details

    • HiddenChainAttackBehaviorParameterTest

      public HiddenChainAttackBehaviorParameterTest()

  • Method Details

    • setUp

      @BeforeEach public void setUp()

    • testConstructor_NullNode_ThrowsNullPointerException

      @Test public void testConstructor_NullNode_ThrowsNullPointerException()
      Tests that constructor throws NullPointerException when node is null. Contract: //@ requires node != null; Note: Objects.requireNonNull throws NullPointerException with custom message

    • testConstructor_InitializesToIdleState

      @Test public void testConstructor_InitializesToIdleState()
      Tests that constructor initializes to IDLE state. Contract: //@ ensures currentState == State.IDLE;

    • testGoToMonitoringState_AlreadyMonitoring_ThrowsIllegalStateException

      @Test public void testGoToMonitoringState_AlreadyMonitoring_ThrowsIllegalStateException()
      Tests that goToMonitoringState() throws when already in MONITORING state. Contract: Precondition violated

    • testGoToMonitoringState_GuardsAgainstInvalidTransitions

      @Test public void testGoToMonitoringState_GuardsAgainstInvalidTransitions()
      Tests that goToMonitoringState() throws when in ATTACKING state. Note: Direct state change to ATTACKING not possible via public API; tested through state transition guard.

    • testGoToIdleState_AlreadyIdle_ThrowsIllegalStateException

      @Test public void testGoToIdleState_AlreadyIdle_ThrowsIllegalStateException()
      Tests that goToIdleState() throws when already in IDLE state. Contract: Precondition violated

    • testGoToIdleState_GuardsAgainstInvalidTransitions

      @Test public void testGoToIdleState_GuardsAgainstInvalidTransitions()
      Tests that goToIdleState() throws when called inappropriately. Can only transition from MONITORING to IDLE.

    • testSetAttackPower_NonPositive_ThrowsIllegalArgumentException

      @Test public void testSetAttackPower_NonPositive_ThrowsIllegalArgumentException()
      Tests that setAttackPower() throws when power is invalid input: '<'= 0. Contract: //@ requires power > 0;

    • testSetAttackPower_Positive_Succeeds

      @Test public void testSetAttackPower_Positive_Succeeds()
      Tests that setAttackPower() accepts positive values. Contract: //@ requires power > 0;

    • testSetReleaseAdvantage_AcceptsAnyValue

      @Test public void testSetReleaseAdvantage_AcceptsAnyValue()
      Tests that setReleaseAdvantage() accepts any value. Note: Unlike setter validation, this setter does not validate the value. Validation occurs in startAttack() via validateAttackParameters().

    • testStateTransition_IdleToMonitoring_Succeeds

      @Test public void testStateTransition_IdleToMonitoring_Succeeds()
      Tests that valid state transition IDLE → MONITORING succeeds.

    • testStateTransition_MonitoringToIdle_Succeeds

      @Test public void testStateTransition_MonitoringToIdle_Succeeds()
      Tests that valid state transition MONITORING → IDLE succeeds.

    • testInHiddenChain_NoAttackInProgress_ReturnsFalse

      @Test public void testInHiddenChain_NoAttackInProgress_ReturnsFalse()
      When no attack is in progress (hiddenChainTip is null), inHiddenChain() must return false for any transaction regardless of its ID. Contract: //@ ensures hiddenChainTip == null ==> \result == false;

    • testInHiddenChain_MonitoringState_ReturnsFalse

      @Test public void testInHiddenChain_MonitoringState_ReturnsFalse()
      When in MONITORING state (attack not yet started, hidden chain empty), inHiddenChain() must return false. Contract: //@ ensures hiddenChainTip == null ==> \result == false;

    • testInHiddenChain_TransactionInTipBlock_ReturnsTrue

      @Test public void testInHiddenChain_TransactionInTipBlock_ReturnsTrue()
      A transaction contained in the single hidden block at the tip must be found. Constructs a hidden chain tip manually via cancelAttack-safe field injection using a single Block containing a known transaction. Contract: //@ ensures (\exists Block b; hiddenChain.contains(b); b.contains(tx)) ==> \result == true;

    • testInHiddenChain_TransactionNotInAnyBlock_ReturnsFalse

      @Test public void testInHiddenChain_TransactionNotInAnyBlock_ReturnsFalse()
      A transaction that has never been seen by the node must not be found in the hidden chain, even during an active attack. Contract: //@ ensures (\forall Block b; hiddenChain.contains(b); !b.contains(tx)) ==> \result == false;

    • testInHiddenChain_AfterCancelAttack_ReturnsFalse

      @Test public void testInHiddenChain_AfterCancelAttack_ReturnsFalse()
      After the attack is cancelled the hidden chain is cleared, so inHiddenChain() must return false for any transaction. Contract: //@ ensures (after cancelAttack) hiddenChainTip == null ==> \result == false;
